activities.name: movable sticky-note responses
activities.instructions: Drag each sticky note into the Notice, Wonder, or Predict space, then explain one choice in a complete sentence.
activities.answer_check: Each sticky note should connect to a real detail from the source image, figure, or prompt.

activities.name: gallery walk sticky-note sort
activities.instructions: Move the sticky notes into the box that best matches your thinking, then add one sentence that uses a clue from the source slide.
activities.answer_check: Each sticky note should match the source image, figure, or problem setup and be supported by a visible detail.

activities.name: cover-and-reveal
activities.instructions: Move one cover at a time, pause after each reveal, and revise your thinking with a partner.
activities.answer_check: Use a clue from the source slide after each reveal to justify your updated idea.

activities.name: estimate-then-reveal prediction
activities.instructions: Make an estimate first, then move the reveal cards to compare your prediction to the source information.
activities.answer_check: Your final statement should name one clue that made your estimate more accurate.

activities.name: card flip reveal discussion
activities.instructions: Flip or move one card at a time to reveal new information and discuss what changes in your reasoning.
activities.answer_check: Each new card should make your explanation more specific and better supported.

activities.name: movable discussion marker selection
activities.instructions: Place a marker next to the goal or strategy that feels strongest before the lesson and again after the lesson.
activities.answer_check: Your after marker should match the notes, speaking, or practice evidence you can point to.

activities.name: strategy ranking activity
activities.instructions: Rank the strategies from most helpful to least helpful for this lesson problem, then defend one ranking choice.
activities.answer_check: Your ranking should match the structure of the source problem and the worked example.

activities.name: visual vocabulary matching
activities.instructions: Match each lesson word to the visual cue, diagram, or representation that helps explain it.
activities.answer_check: Each match should connect the word to something visible in the source slides.

activities.name: definition matching
activities.instructions: Pair each lesson word with the student-friendly definition that fits it best.
activities.answer_check: Read each pair aloud to be sure the definition sounds right in a full sentence.

activities.name: math vocabulary categorization
activities.instructions: Sort the lesson words into categories such as shape words, operation words, and explanation words.
activities.answer_check: Your categories should help you talk and write more precisely about the source problem.

activities.name: algebra vocabulary placement
activities.instructions: Place each word next to the part of the model, diagram, or representation where it belongs.
activities.answer_check: Every placement should make the labeled diagram easier to read and explain.

activities.name: concept map building
activities.instructions: Use the cards to build a concept map that shows how the lesson ideas connect.
activities.answer_check: The connections should follow the order and meaning of the source slides.

activities.name: flowchart completion
activities.instructions: Place the steps into the flowchart so the process matches the lesson sequence.
activities.answer_check: Each step should lead naturally to the next without skipping the source reasoning.

activities.name: label-the-diagram placement
activities.instructions: Drag each label to the correct part of the figure, then explain why the label belongs there.
activities.answer_check: Check that every label matches the source figure and lesson wording.

activities.name: model annotation placement
activities.instructions: Place each annotation where it belongs on the model, then explain how the annotation helps you solve or understand the source problem.
activities.answer_check: Every annotation should make the source model clearer and should match the exact figure, labels, or quantities from the slides.

activities.name: build-a-model representation
activities.instructions: Use the movable pieces to build the model or representation shown in the source lesson.
activities.answer_check: Compare your built model to the source example and fix any missing or misplaced pieces.

activities.name: step sequencing
activities.instructions: Move the cards into the correct order so the worked example follows the lesson exactly.
activities.answer_check: Each step should be necessary and should connect clearly to the next step.

activities.name: reasoning ladder build
activities.instructions: Build the ladder from starting idea to final explanation so your reasoning is complete and easy to follow.
activities.answer_check: Each rung should show a clear move in the thinking and should connect logically to the next rung.

activities.name: equation builder
activities.instructions: Build the equation or expression from the lesson using the movable cards, then explain what each part represents.
activities.answer_check: Each piece should match the quantities and labels in the source problem.

activities.name: step-by-step solution reconstruction
activities.instructions: Reconstruct the full solution path in order, using the source language from the worked example.
activities.answer_check: Your reconstructed path should match the original strategy and final result.

activities.name: missing-step detective
activities.instructions: Find the missing step or missing reason, then add it back using evidence from the source example.
activities.answer_check: The missing step should make the worked example complete and logical again.

activities.name: error analysis repair cards
activities.instructions: Match the error to the repair card, then explain how the corrected reasoning fits the source model or problem.
activities.answer_check: The repair should fix the exact error and should produce a solution path that matches the source lesson.

activities.name: drag-and-sort categorization
activities.instructions: Sort the problem cards into categories such as known information, strategy, and solution idea.
activities.answer_check: Every card should fit the category rule and help you solve the source problem.

activities.name: strategy sort mat
activities.instructions: Sort the cards by which strategy or move they support, then use one sorted set to explain your plan for the source problem.
activities.answer_check: Each card should belong with the strategy it truly supports, and your explanation should match the source task.

activities.name: matching pairs
activities.instructions: Match each problem card to the representation, step, or explanation that fits it best.
activities.answer_check: Each pair should show the same math idea in two connected forms.

activities.name: claim-evidence match
activities.instructions: Match each claim or answer choice to the evidence that proves it, then explain why the pair belongs together.
activities.answer_check: Every claim should be backed by a specific detail from the source problem, model, or representation.

activities.name: table-to-equation matching
activities.instructions: Match each table, figure, or value set to the equation or statement that represents it.
activities.answer_check: Check that the relationship stays true across all matched parts.

activities.name: representation matching (table/graph/equation)
activities.instructions: Connect the matching representations and explain how each one shows the same idea.
activities.answer_check: The matched set should agree on the quantities, labels, and final interpretation.

activities.name: build-the-table activity
activities.instructions: Build the missing table or organizer from the source information before solving.
activities.answer_check: Every entry should match the source labels, values, and relationships.

activities.name: true/false sorting
activities.instructions: Sort each statement into true or false, then fix one false statement in your own words.
activities.answer_check: Use the lesson notes or source example to justify each sort.

activities.name: four-corner claim sort
activities.instructions: Place each claim in the corner that best matches your confidence, then defend one placement with evidence from the lesson.
activities.answer_check: Your placement should match what the source notes, model, or practice actually show.

activities.name: peer answer check placement
activities.instructions: Place the check markers next to the work or objective that has clear evidence, then explain your choice.
activities.answer_check: A check should only go where you can point to notes, speaking, or written work as proof.

activities.name: multi-step problem path selection
activities.instructions: Choose and order the best path through the multi-step problem, then justify why that path is efficient.
activities.answer_check: Your path should use all necessary information from the source challenge and no unnecessary steps.

activities.name: claim-evidence sort mat
activities.instructions: Sort each claim under the evidence that proves or weakens it, then write a final defense for your best-supported choice.
activities.answer_check: The strongest claim should be supported by evidence that clearly comes from the source challenge and not from guessing.

activities.name: drag-to-justify reasoning
activities.instructions: Move each evidence card next to the claim or solution choice it supports, then write one justification sentence.
activities.answer_check: Every claim should be backed by a specific detail from the source problem or model.

activities.name: build-the-argument reasoning cards
activities.instructions: Arrange the cards to build a complete argument with a claim, evidence, and reasoning.
activities.answer_check: Your argument should clearly connect the evidence to the final claim.

activities.name: reflection evidence sort
activities.instructions: Sort the evidence cards by what helped you most, then use the strongest evidence to explain what you learned.
activities.answer_check: Your strongest evidence should connect directly to the lesson model, practice, or discussion.

activities.name: mathematical debate claim placement
activities.instructions: Place the claim cards under the choice you support most strongly, then defend your position with evidence.
activities.answer_check: The strongest claim should match the actual structure and quantities in the source task.

activities.name: sentence builder
activities.instructions: Build a complete reflection sentence using the lesson vocabulary and evidence cards.
activities.answer_check: Your final sentence should include both what you learned and why it makes sense.

activities.name: build-a-math-explanation sentence strips
activities.instructions: Arrange the sentence strips to build a clear math explanation, then read it aloud to check fluency.
activities.answer_check: The explanation should sound complete, precise, and tied to the source problem.

activities.name: discussion evidence placement
activities.instructions: Place each evidence card next to the idea it supports, then explain one match with a partner.
activities.answer_check: Your evidence should come from the actual notes, model, or solved problem.

activities.name: reasoning ladder building
activities.instructions: Build the reasoning ladder from basic idea to full explanation using the lesson vocabulary.
activities.answer_check: Each rung should make your explanation more complete and more precise.

activities.name: check-the-solution placement
activities.instructions: Place the check cards next to the part of the work that proves the answer is correct.
activities.answer_check: Your checks should point to computation, units, labels, or reasoning that verifies the result.

activities.name: equation error correction drag
activities.instructions: Drag the correction card to the step with the mistake, then explain how to fix it.
activities.answer_check: The corrected work should now match the source relationship and final answer.

activities.name: mathematical error explanation placement
activities.instructions: Place the explanation cards next to the error they describe, then write the corrected reasoning.
activities.answer_check: Your explanation should identify both what went wrong and what should happen instead.

activities.name: show-what-you-know self-check
activities.instructions: Move each check to the part of your work that shows the lesson goal, then add one sentence explaining why your evidence is strong.
activities.answer_check: A check should only be placed where the work clearly matches the source strategy, vocabulary, or final reasoning.

activities.name: area model construction
activities.instructions: Use the pieces to build an area model that matches the dimensions and labels from the source figure.
activities.answer_check: The finished model should show all needed parts of the area calculation clearly.

activities.name: geometric figure property sorting
activities.instructions: Sort the figure cards by their properties, then explain which property matters most for solving the lesson problem.
activities.answer_check: Your sort should highlight the properties actually used in the source solution.
